Abbreviated Title: An Intrusion-Detection Model
Key Contribution: Founded anomaly-based intrusion monitoring using statistical profiles on audit trails
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Theoretical basis for behaviour-based anomaly detection; ancestral motivation for Isolation Forest use

Abbreviated Title: Guidelines on Firewalls and Firewall Policy (NIST SP 800-41)
Key Contribution: Authoritative taxonomy of firewall types: packet filter, stateful, proxy, NGFW
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Provides the firewall classification framework used in comparative analysis

Abbreviated Title: A History and Survey of Network Firewalls
Key Contribution: Traced development from screend and Bell Labs gateways through stateful inspection
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Contextualises the trajectory from stateless to stateful to adaptive enforcement

Abbreviated Title: Next Generation Firewall for Network Security: A Survey
Key Contribution: Comprehensive NGFW taxonomy; analysis of DPI, App-ID, threat intelligence integration
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Core framework for understanding what NGFW capabilities OPNsense currently lacks

Abbreviated Title: Evolution of Firewalls: Toward Securer Network Using NGFW
Key Contribution: Analysed UTM-to-NGFW transition; single-pass architecture advantages over multi-engine UTM
Methodology: Case study of commercial NGFW architectures
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Informs sidecar design rationale: avoiding added per-packet overhead on OPNsense

Abbreviated Title: NGFW in Endpoint Security
Key Contribution: TLS 1.3 and QUIC adoption reduce DPI effectiveness; encrypted traffic challenge for signature-based systems
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Motivates flow-feature approach: filterlog metadata is encryption-agnostic

Abbreviated Title: Software-Based DPI for Real-Time Anomaly Detection
Key Contribution: Software DPI achieves real-time anomaly detection on commodity hardware without dedicated ASICs
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Establishes feasibility of meaningful ML detection on the commodity Ubuntu control node

Abbreviated Title: Comparative Study of Commercial NGFWs
Key Contribution: NGFWs outperform conventional firewalls on encrypted attacks; performance-security tradeoff significant for constrained deployments
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Justifies sidecar placement on separate host to avoid adding OPNsense processing load

Abbreviated Title: NGFW Market and Open-Source Gap Analysis
Key Contribution: Adaptive context-driven threat prevention identified as exclusively commercial-vendor feature
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Direct citation for the ecosystem gap the BRB-NGFW project addresses

Abbreviated Title: Network Intrusion Detection System: A Systematic Study
Key Contribution: Deep learning achieves higher accuracy on benchmarks; traditional ML competitive for real-time detection
Methodology: Systematic review of ML/DL classifiers on benchmark datasets
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Justifies lightweight Isolation Forest over deep learning for commodity-hardware deployment

Abbreviated Title: Comparative Study of Ensemble Methods for NIDS
Key Contribution: Ensemble methods outperform individual classifiers but generalise poorly across datasets
Methodology: Empirical benchmarking on CICIDS2017 and NSL-KDD
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Motivates training on environment-specific lab data rather than public benchmark datasets

Abbreviated Title: Machine Learning-Based IDS: A Survey
Key Contribution: Unsupervised methods are most relevant when labelled attack data is unavailable or evolving
Methodology: Survey of supervised, semi-supervised, and unsupervised IDS
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Supports choice of unsupervised Isolation Forest; no labelled attack data required

Abbreviated Title: Survey of Intrusion Detection Systems
Key Contribution: False positive rate is the dominant barrier to production IDS deployment
Methodology: Survey of IDS techniques, datasets, and operational challenges
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Informs contamination parameter choice and motivates operator-visible rather than fully autonomous response

Abbreviated Title: Overview on IDS Design Exploiting ML for Cybersecurity
Key Contribution: Three barriers to deployment: feature engineering quality, dataset fidelity, integration complexity
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Motivates sidecar architecture as solution to integration complexity barrier

Abbreviated Title: Leveraging Deep Neural Networks for Anomaly-Based Web IDS
Key Contribution: Deep NNs effectively classify L7 HTTP traffic as legitimate or malicious
Methodology: Deep learning on HTTP request features
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Establishes feasibility of extending feature set to L7 in future project iterations

Abbreviated Title: Machine Learning for IoT Security
Key Contribution: Resource constraints consistently favour lightweight algorithms over deep learning
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Confirms Isolation Forest suitability for virtualised lab environment

Abbreviated Title: Isolation Forest
Key Contribution: Proposed linear-time unsupervised anomaly detection via random recursive partitioning
Methodology: Ensemble of isolation trees with path-length anomaly scoring
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Core algorithm implemented in ml/train.py with n_estimators=200, contamination=0.05

Abbreviated Title: Anomaly Detection: A Survey
Key Contribution: Comprehensive taxonomy of anomaly types (point, contextual, collective) and detection approaches
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Theoretical foundation for classifying network anomalies; point anomaly framing applied in this project

Abbreviated Title: Isolation Forest: A Survey of Applications
Key Contribution: Reviewed 100+ Isolation Forest applications; consistently competitive performance at lower cost than alternatives
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Validates IF effectiveness on network data; supports n_estimators choice

Abbreviated Title: Parallel Isolation Forest for Network Traffic Anomaly Detection
Key Contribution: Validated Isolation Forest on real-world network traffic at scale using Spark
Methodology: Parallel IF on Apache Spark with flow-level features (rates, inter-arrival, protocol)
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Confirms filterlog feature set (proto, dst_port, direction, action) aligns with validated feature categories

Abbreviated Title: Outlier Detection: IF vs LOF vs One-Class SVM
Key Contribution: Isolation Forest substantially faster than LOF and one-class SVM at comparable accuracy
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Justifies IF selection over alternatives given inference latency constraint in the lab

Abbreviated Title: Adversarial Attacks on ML-Based IDS
Key Contribution: ML detection systems including IF are vulnerable to adversarial feature manipulation
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Identifies future robustness work; acknowledged as current implementation limitation

Abbreviated Title: Behaviour-Based Security in Open-Source Firewalls
Key Contribution: pfSense and OPNsense limited to signature-based detection; no adaptive capability
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Directly establishes the open-source ecosystem gap BRB-NGFW fills

Abbreviated Title: Benchmarking Open-Source Firewall Platforms
Key Contribution: OPNsense and pfSense outperform IPFire and Endian in throughput; no ML integration in any platform
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Confirms OPNsense as performance-adequate baseline; no ML capability confirmed

Abbreviated Title: OPNsense Architecture and REST API Analysis
Key Contribution: OPNsense REST API sufficient for alias management, rule config, and state querying to support external orchestration
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Direct reference for the alias_util endpoints used in control-api/app.py

Abbreviated Title: Modern Cybersecurity Architecture Trends
Key Contribution: Convergence of threat intelligence, behavioural analytics, and automated response identified as defining NGFW trend
Methodology: Survey of commercial and research architectures
Relevance to BRB-NGFW: Provides evaluation framework: detection scope, response latency, adaptation speed
